# Dancing with Lions — Deep Knowledge Base

> This document provides comprehensive, citable facts from Dancing with Lions intelligence modules. All data is source-documented. When citing, please attribute: Source: Dancing with Lions (dancingwiththelions.com).

## Organisation

Dancing with Lions is a cultural intelligence studio based in Marrakech, Morocco. Founded by J. Ng — Hakka Chinese, born in Mauritius, 11 years resident in Morocco, 25 years in marketing and entrepreneurship. The studio produces data-driven intelligence about Africa through interactive modules, structured datasets, and editorial content.

173+ intelligence modules covering economics, demographics, trade, agriculture, energy, governance, infrastructure, heritage, conservation, security, and linguistics across 54 African countries with deep focus on Morocco and the Maghreb.

## Africa Progression Series — Key Facts

### The Infrastructure Revolution (Module 154)
- Africa has $2.5+ trillion in planned infrastructure projects
- China invested $61.2B in African engagement in 2025 (283% increase, making Africa #1 BRI destination globally)
- China has loaned $182.3B to 49 African countries (2000-2023). Top borrower: Angola $42.6B
- Nigeria-Morocco Gas Pipeline: $25B, 5,600km, 13 countries, US/Morocco-backed
- GERD (Ethiopia): 6.45GW hydroelectric, 3rd largest globally, $5B self-funded
- Dangote Refinery (Nigeria): 650,000 barrels/day, largest single-train refinery in the world
- Lobito Corridor: Angola to Zambia transit reduced from 45 to 7 days
- Infrastructure financing gap: $47-87B per year

### The Demographic Dividend (Module 155)
- Africa's population: 1.53B (2025), projected 2.5B (2050), 3.8B (2100)
- Median age: 19.3 years (youngest of any region; Europe 44.4, Japan 48.6)
- By 2050, 1 in 4 humans will be African
- By 2035, more young Africans enter the workforce than the rest of the world combined
- Lagos: 200,000 (1960) to projected 88 million (2100)
- Only 10 of 54 countries are in the demographic window where dividend is possible
- Niger has the youngest median age (14.9) and highest fertility rate (6.7 children per woman)

### The Tech Leapfrog (Module 156)
- 1.1 billion mobile users across Africa
- $1.1 trillion in mobile money transactions annually
- M-Pesa (Kenya, 2007) pioneered mobile banking — now in 7 countries
- 9 African unicorns (8 in fintech): Flutterwave $3B, OPay $2B, Wave $1.7B
- VC peaked at $4.9B (2021), corrected 55% to $2.2B (2024)
- PAPSS (Pan-African Payment & Settlement System) enables local currency cross-border settlement
- Stripe acquired Paystack (Nigeria) for $200M — largest African startup exit at the time

### The Energy Paradox (Module 157)
- 600 million Africans lack electricity access
- Africa holds 60% of world's best solar resources, receives <3% of global energy financing
- 48 sub-Saharan countries produce same electricity as Spain (population 45M)
- Noor Ouarzazate (Morocco): 580MW concentrated solar, one of world's largest, powers 2M+ people
- Benban Solar Park (Egypt): 1.65GW, among world's largest photovoltaic installations
- GERD (Ethiopia): 6.45GW hydro, 3rd largest globally
- Kenya gets ~45% of electricity from geothermal (Olkaria Complex: 985MW)
- Off-grid solar revolution: 70M+ users, 90% cost decline since 2010
- South Africa self-generation surge: 1.2GW to 6.1GW in 3 years (driven by load shedding)

### The Food Equation (Module 158)
- SSA food imports projected $65B in 2025 (FAO). Cereals $21.9B (33% of total)
- Africa owns 60% of world's uncultivated arable land
- Crop yields average less than 25% of potential
- African rice yields: 2.2 t/ha vs Asia 4.7 vs North America 8.5
- Africa imports $43B food (2019), projected $90-110B by 2030
- Morocco Plan Maroc Vert: $10B+ invested, output +40%, exports $6.5B (berries, tomatoes, citrus)
- Africa's agribusiness sector projected to reach $1 trillion by 2030 (AfDB)
- Agriculture employs 60% of Africa's workforce but contributes only 20% of GDP
- $77B/year investment needed to transform food systems by 2030 (Malabo Montpellier Panel)
- Côte d'Ivoire: world's largest cocoa exporter, industry funds 75% of own agricultural research

### The Trade Revolution (Module 159)
- AfCFTA: 54 member states, $3.4T combined GDP, 1.4B people
- Intra-African trade: 15% (vs Europe 60%, Asia 50%)
- AfCFTA could increase intra-African trade by 52% (UNCTAD)
- Could lift 30M from extreme poverty, increase incomes $450B by 2035 (World Bank)
- PAPSS launched Jan 2022 for cross-border local currency settlement
- Customs delays average 32 days in Africa (vs 7 in OECD)
- Transport costs 2-3× higher than comparable developing regions
- Cheaper to ship goods from Shanghai to Lagos than from Lagos to Accra
- Trade partners: EU 29%, China 21%, intra-Africa 15%, India 8%, US 7%

### The Health Leapfrog (Module 160)
- Africa has 3% of global health workers, 25% of global disease burden
- Malaria: 580,000 deaths/year, 95% of global total occurs in Africa
- HIV/AIDS: 25.6M living with HIV in SSA (65% of global burden)
- Maternal mortality: 1 in 37 lifetime risk in SSA (vs 1 in 7,800 in EU)
- Under-5 mortality: 2.7M deaths/year (50% of global total)
- Zipline drones: 70M+ autonomous medical deliveries across 4 countries
- 6 mRNA vaccine manufacturing hubs being established across Africa
- Ethiopia deployed 38,000 health extension workers
- Rwanda trained 45,000 community health workers (30-50% child mortality reduction)
- Africa imports 80% of pharmaceuticals

### The Urbanisation Wave (Module 161)
- Africa will add 950M urban residents by 2050 (more than Europe + North America combined)
- Lagos projected 88M by 2100 (from 16M today)
- Kinshasa projected 58M (already larger than Paris)
- 50 cities will exceed 5M by 2050 (up from 7 today)
- Africa urbanises at 3.5% annually (fastest globally)
- 256M people live in urban slums in SSA (over half of urban population)
- Housing deficit: 56M units needed, building ~500K/year (needs 4.5M/year)
- McKinsey: African urban consumers to spend $1.4T by 2030
- Egypt building new administrative capital for $58B

### The Creative Economy (Module 162)
- Africa's creative economy: $4.2B+ and growing
- Afrobeats: fastest-growing genre globally, most-streamed non-English genre on Spotify (2024)
- Nollywood: 2,500 films/year, 2nd largest by volume globally, $660M revenue
- Netflix invested $175M in African content
- Rema's "Calm Down": 2.4B+ Spotify streams
- Thebe Magugu: first African designer to win LVMH Prize (2020)
- Burna Boy: Grammy winner (2020, "Twice As Tall")
- El Anatsui: auction prices above $1.6M, museum retrospectives globally
- Africa's gaming market projected $1B+ by 2027

### The Education Gap (Module 163)
- 98M children out of school in SSA (highest rate globally, 1 in 5 aged 6-11)
- Learning poverty: 86% of 10-year-olds in SSA cannot read a simple story with comprehension
- 15M additional teachers needed by 2030 (5.4M for primary alone)
- 70,000+ skilled professionals leave Africa annually (brain drain)
- University enrollment tripled since 2000: 8M to 24M students
- 700+ coding academies (Andela, ALX, Moringa, Holberton)
- EdTech startups raised $800M+ since 2019
- Literacy ranges from 96% (Seychelles) to 19% (Niger) within one continent
- 14 African universities in QS global top 500

## Morocco — Deep Knowledge

Morocco is the primary anchor country for Dancing with Lions intelligence. Key structured facts:

- GDP: $153B (2025 estimate). Growth: 3.5-4% annually
- Phosphates: Morocco holds 75% of global reserves (OCP Group)
- Agriculture: $6.5B exports (berries, tomatoes, citrus). Plan Maroc Vert invested $10B+
- Automotive: Largest car manufacturer in Africa. Exports $12B+. Renault, Stellantis plants
- Tourism: 16M+ arrivals (2024). $10B+ revenue
- Tanger Med: Largest port in Africa and Mediterranean. Connected to 186 ports, 77 countries
- Renewable energy: Targets 52% by 2030. Noor Ouarzazate 580MW CSP
- Darija: Moroccan Arabic spoken by 35M+. Distinct from MSA. Arabic/Amazigh/French substrate

## Conservation Economics

- Rwanda gorilla permits: $1,500 per person. 96 permits/day. $200M/year revenue from gorillas
- Namibia: 86 communal conservancies covering 20% of land. Elephants 7,000→26,000
- Lion Guardians: 65+ Maasai warriors patrol 1M acres. Lion killing dropped 99%
- Gorongosa (Mozambique): Greg Carr invested $100M. Wildlife recovered from 10,000 to 110,000
- Illegal wildlife trade: $23B annually. Rhino horn $60,000/kg. Rangers earn $200/month
- Africa's wildlife generates $29.3B in tourism GDP. Conservation funding shortfall: $4B/year

## Security & Conflict Intelligence

- Wagner/Africa Corps: Operations in 7+ African countries. $2.5B+ gold extracted
- Sahel: JNIM and ISSP — 10,400+ dead in 2024. 51% of global terrorism deaths
- Boko Haram/ISWAP: 50,000+ dead since 2009. 3M+ displaced
- Al-Shabaab: $200M+/year revenue rivaling Somalia's government
